perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means: (i) technologies in written or tangible form disclosed to COMPANY from time to time after the Effective Date of this Agreement by ROCHESTER and identified with particularity at time of disclosure and marked co nfidential; and (ii) technolo gies communicated orally or visually to COMPANY by ROCH ESTER, if it is reduced to writing or other tangible form by ROCHESTER on or before the date thirty days after the date of such communication, marked confidential, and promptly delivered to COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means: technologies disclosed to COMPANY from time to time after the Effective Date of this Agreement by ROCHESTER.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of Confidential Information included specific requirements for written or tangible forms, identification at the time of disclosure, and marking as confidential. For orally or visually communicated technologies, it required reduction to writing within 30 days and prompt delivery. By removing these requirements, the definition is now vague. This creates an in-text contradiction, especially in Section 3, which refers to 'receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement,' as it is now unclear when 'receipt' occurs since the original requirements for confidential information to be considered valid have been removed, making the enforcement of the agreement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY agree that, for a period of five (5) ye ars after the date of its receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement, it shall: (i) keep Confidential Information confidential; and (ii) not use the Confidential Information for any commercial purpose. The foregoing shall not apply to that part of any Confidential Information that: (a) is disclosed or used by COMPANY in a ccordance with any written consent granted by ROCHESTER; or (b) at the time of receipt by COMPANY wa s independently know n by COMPANY; or (c) at any time becomes generally known to the public through no fault of COMPANY; or (d) has been or is made available to COMPANY by a third party having the lawful right to do so without breaching any obligation of nonuse or confidentiality to ROCHESTER; or (e) has been or is disclosed to others by ROCHESTER without similar restrictions on disclosure and use; or (f) COMPANY is required to disclose pursuant to an order of a judicial or administrative authority
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a specific definition of 'Confidential Information', including requirements for it to be marked confidential and, for oral communications, reduced to writing within 30 days. The changed text removes these requirements, making the definition broader and less precise. The contradicted text, Section 3, refers to 'receipt of the last Confidential Information'. Without the original requirements, it becomes unclear when 'receipt' occurs, creating a contradiction and making enforcement uncertain. Thus, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: After ROCHESTER receives a fully signed copy of this Agreement, ROCHESTER shall disclose to COMPANY Confidential Information solely for use by COMPANY in its internal evaluation of the Confidential Information’s commercial prospects.
perturbation.changed_text: After ROCHESTER receives a fully signed copy of this Agreement, ROCHESTER may disclose information to COMPANY for use by COMPANY in its internal evaluation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall disclose Confidential Information' to 'may disclose information', the obligation of ROCHESTER to disclose any Confidential Information is removed. This contradicts the very purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement, which is to allow for the sharing of confidential information, and contradicts section 3 as there is no actual obligation for Rochester to send confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY agree that, for a period of five (5) ye ars after the date of its receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement, it shall: (i) keep Confidential Information confidential; and (ii) not use the Confidential Information for any commercial purpose. The foregoing shall not apply to that part of any Confidential Information that: (a) is disclosed or used by COMPANY in a ccordance with any written consent granted by ROCHESTER; or (b) at the time of receipt by COMPANY wa s independently know n by COMPANY; or (c) at any time becomes generally known to the public through no fault of COMPANY; or (d) has been or is made available to COMPANY by a third party having the lawful right to do so without breaching any obligation of nonuse or confidentiality to ROCHESTER; or (e) has been or is disclosed to others by ROCHESTER without similar restrictions on disclosure and use; or (f) COMPANY is required to disclose pursuant to an order of a judicial or administrative authority
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that ROCHESTER 'shall disclose' Confidential Information, creating an obligation. The changed text replaces this with 'may disclose information', removing the obligation. The contradicted text refers to 'receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement', which implies that ROCHESTER is obligated to provide information. By changing 'shall' to 'may', there is no guarantee that Confidential Information will be disclosed, thus contradicting the assumption in the contradicted text that information will be received.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY shall complete its evaluation within the period expiring on the date six (6) months after the date COMPANY receives the Confidential Information from ROCHESTER.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY shall complete its evaluation within a reasonable period.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specified six-month evaluation period creates ambiguity. This weakens the agreement because 'reasonable period' is subjective and difficult to enforce. Section 6 now becomes contradictory, as the term 'Evaluation Period' is still referenced, however there is no explicit mention on how long this period will take place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the end of the Evaluation Period, COMPANY shall upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all Confidential Information in its files to monitor its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a clear six-month evaluation period. The changed text replaces this with 'a reasonable period,' creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to a defined 'Evaluation Period.' Without a specified duration, the instruction to return Confidential Information 'at the end of the Evaluation Period' becomes unenforceable, fitting the definition of an 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: At the end of the Evaluation Period, COMPANY shall upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all Confidential Information in its files to monitor its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY shall, upon request of ROCHESTER, return to ROCHESTER all information in its files.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'At the end of the Evaluation Period' and 'Confidential Information,' the obligation to return information is no longer tied to a specific timeframe or type of information. This contradicts Section 3, which specifically outlines obligations regarding 'Confidential Information.' Now, section 6 obligation is to return all information, not only that of confidential nature, which is not aligned with the initial definition and purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY agree that, for a period of five (5) ye ars after the date of its receipt of the last Confidential Information disclosed under the Agreement, it shall: (i) keep Confidential Information confidential; and (ii) not use the Confidential Information for any commercial purpose. The foregoing shall not apply to that part of any Confidential Information that: (a) is disclosed or used by COMPANY in a ccordance with any written consent granted by ROCHESTER; or (b) at the time of receipt by COMPANY wa s independently know n by COMPANY; or (c) at any time becomes generally known to the public through no fault of COMPANY; or (d) has been or is made available to COMPANY by a third party having the lawful right to do so without breaching any obligation of nonuse or confidentiality to ROCHESTER; or (e) has been or is disclosed to others by ROCHESTER without similar restrictions on disclosure and use; or (f) COMPANY is required to disclose pursuant to an order of a judicial or administrative authority
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the return of information to 'Confidential Information' at the end of the 'Evaluation Period.' The changed text broadens this obligation to include 'all information' at any time, not only at the end of the evaluation period. This contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies restrictions and obligations specifically related to 'Confidential Information' and a time period of 5 years following receipt of confidential information. The changed text now requires the return of any and all information, regardless of its confidential status or if the evaluation period has ended, which conflicts with the limitations and scope defined in the contradicted section, making the latter unenforceable or at least ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be construed to grant to COMPANY any express or implied option, license or other right, title, or interest in or to the Confidential Information , or the patent rights corresponding to the Confidential Information, or obligate either party to enter in to any agreement granting any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not be construed to grant to COMPANY any express or implied option, license or other right, title, or interest, or obligate either party to enter in to any agreement granting any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in or to the Confidential Information, or the patent rights corresponding to the Confidential Information', it creates an ambiguity that could be interpreted as granting rights not directly tied to the disclosed information. This is contradictory to the implied understanding of an NDA, as it no longer explicitly protects confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information means: technologies disclosed to COMPANY from time to time after the Effective Date of this Agreement by ROCHESTER.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically excludes granting rights to the Confidential Information or related patents. The changed text removes this explicit exclusion, potentially opening the door for a broader interpretation that could grant rights related to confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines confidential information as technologies disclosed by ROCHESTER to COMPANY. By removing the explicit protection of confidential information, it introduces a contradiction regarding the scope of rights granted under the agreement, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement of the parties relating to Confidential Information , and any corresponding patent rights, and any and all contemporaneous and prior oral and written understandings and agreements with respect thereto are superseded by this document.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement of the parties, and any and all contemporaneous and prior oral and written understandings and agreements with respect thereto are superseded by this document.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'relating to Confidential Information , and any corresponding patent rights' narrows the scope of the agreement. It may now be argued that aspects related to the confidential information are not entirely covered by this agreement, contradicting the main intention of a Non-Disclosure Agreement and making it open for other types of interpreations. This contradiction the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: After ROCHESTER receives a fully signed copy of this Agreement, ROCHESTER may disclose information to COMPANY for use by COMPANY in its internal evaluation.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly covers 'Confidential Information, and any corresponding patent rights.' The changed text omits this phrase, narrowing the agreement's scope. The contradicted text states 'ROCHESTER may disclose information to COMPANY for use by COMPANY in its internal evaluation' after the agreement is signed. By removing the specific reference to 'Confidential Information, and any corresponding patent rights,' the altered text creates uncertainty about whether disclosed information related to confidential information and patent rights are fully covered by the agreement. This directly contradicts the expectation set in the contradicted text that such disclosures are governed by a comprehensive agreement.
